what_it_does: This file is a metadata-only channel attachment record that links an attachment identifier to a board item via a board:// URI.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format with timezone.
The attachment id is 'att-001894bd' and the channel is 'dragnet-development'.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference a board item.
The file is a pure metadata stub with no payload or explanatory text.
The attachment_type 'board_items' signals a cross-reference to a board/kanban item.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added.
References external entities with a URI-style scheme (board://...).
concerns: No actual attachment content or payload is present in the file.
The referenced board item is not described or included, so the file is not self-explanatory.
No indication of who added the attachment or why.
notes: A bare attachment metadata stub that points to a board item but carries no content of its own.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
What is the subject of board item item-c8d428f5?
Why does the attachment file contain no body or payload?
intention_signals: It appears to be registering a timestamped, typed link between a channel attachment and a board item so that channel memory can reference board-level work items.
novelty: Use of a board:// URI scheme as a target and an attachment record with no body/payload.
contribution: It contributes a lightweight, timestamped, typed index entry that connects a channel attachment identifier to a board item, supporting traceability without embedding the actual content.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML metadata attachment record (id att-001894bd) in the dragnet-development channel that points to board item item-c8d428f5 with attachment type board_items and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to register and timestamp a channel attachment reference to a specific board item for traceability.
